[The World Cup](https://www.foxnews.com/category/sports/soccer/the-world-cup)
USA World Cup star Folarin Balogun available to play vs Belgium in stunning twist
Balogun scored against Bosnia and Herzegovina but was sent off for a red card
By [Ryan Gaydos](https://www.foxnews.com/person/g/ryan-gaydos) [Fox News](https://www.foxnews.com/)
Published [July 5, 2026 1:04pm EDT](https://www.foxnews.com/html-sitemap/2026/july/5)

blocks.alt: Former USA World Cup star wants red card rule changes after Folarin Balogun's suspension

blocks.md: [Video](https://www.foxnews.com/video/6400037826112)

links.text: Former USA World Cup star wants red card rule changes after Folarin Balogun's suspension

blocks.md: [Former USA World Cup star wants red card rule changes after Folarin Balogun's suspension](https://www.foxnews.com/video/6400037826112)
1994 USA World Cup player John Harkes, who was suspended himself for yellow card violations, wants to see FIFA change its red card rules after Folarin Balogun's suspension.

USA World Cup star Folarin Balogun will be able to play in Monday’s game [against Belgium](https://www.foxnews.com/outkick-sports/world-cup-round-32-soccer-preview-england-belgium-usa-seek-regulation-wins-packed-day) in the Round of 16 match after all following an uproar over a red card he received against Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Balogun received the red card after he stepped on the foot of Bosnia and Herzegovina defender Tarik Muharemovic. The red card was given after Brazilian referee Raphael Claus stopped the match for a VAR review to determine whether Balogun had committed a severe foul.

links.text: **ZERO BS. JUST DAKICH. TAKE THE DON'T @ ME PODCAST ON THE ROAD. DOWNLOAD NOW!**

blocks.md: [**ZERO BS. JUST DAKICH. TAKE THE DON'T @ ME PODCAST ON THE ROAD. DOWNLOAD NOW!**](https://link.podtrac.com/dmwdd_article)
![2283713280](https://a57.foxnews.com/static.foxnews.com/foxnews.com/content/uploads/2026/07/686/384/folarin-balogun-7226.jpg?ve=1&tl=1)

Folarin Balogun of United States of America walks off the field after receiving a red card during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 match between United States of America and Bosnia and Herzegovina on July 1, 2026 at San Francisco Bay Area Stadium in Santa Clara, California (Matthew Huang/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images)
It was ruled that Balogun did and he was sent off.

[The Athletic](https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7423410/2026/07/05/usmnt-folarin-balogun-red-card-available/), which first reported Balogun’s playing status on Sunday, noted that part of FIFA’s disciplinary code stated, "the judicial body may decide to fully or partially suspend the implementation of a disciplinary measure."

"If the person benefiting from a suspended sanction commits another infringement of a similar nature and gravity during the probationary period, the suspension shall be revoked by the judicial body and the sanction enforced without prejudice to any additional sanction imposed for the new infringement," the rule added.

[FOX Sports](https://www.foxsports.com/stories/soccer/usa-star-folarin-balogun-play-world-cup-match-vs-belgium) confirmed that Balogun would be able to play. The report noted that the play should not have been flagged to Claus.

![Folarin Balogun and Christian Pulisic](https://a57.foxnews.com/static.foxnews.com/foxnews.com/content/uploads/2026/07/686/384/world-cup-folarin-balogun-070126-2.jpg?ve=1&tl=1)

United States' Folarin Balogun (20) and United States' Christian Pulisic (10) stand by after Balogun received a red card during the World Cup round of 32 soccer match between the United States and Bosnia in Santa Clara, Calif., near San Francisco, Wednesday, July 1, 2026. (AP Photo/Martin Meissner)

FIFA confirmed that the suspension was suspended for a [probationary period of one year](https://www.foxnews.com/sports/fifa-final-ruling-us-soccer-star-folarin-balogun-controversial-red-card-suspension).

"We accept the decision of the Disciplinary Committee and are pleased that Folarin Balogun is eligible to compete tomorrow," U.S. Soccer said. "Our full attention is focused on the Round of 16 match against Belgium in Seattle, and we look forward to the continued support of our amazing fans."
Balogun scored against Bosnia and Herzegovina. He spoke to reporters Friday about the red card.

"First and foremost, it was totally unintentional, which I'm sure a lot of people know. But, you know, I think the choice of the referee was, of course, his choice, but I don't think it was the correct call," he said. "I think a yellow card would have been fair due to it not being intentional. There's not many places I can place my foot in such a scenario. But it's happened, and it's something that I have to accept and move forward from.

"There’s been lots of [different emotions](https://www.foxnews.com/sports/former-team-usa-star-wants-see-red-card-rule-change-after-falorin-balogun-controversy). I’ve been upset, I’ve been happy, it’s been surreal to be honest," he added. "But for me it was just important to stay calm. I never want to react out of anger and emotion. There’s still lots of people we’re inspiring. Little kids, boys and girls are watching. We have to show them the correct way to handle things even when you think it’s unjust, so I felt I did that. I’m happy with the support, not just I’m receiving but the team. I think we’re all looking forward to seeing what we can do against Belgium."

[U.S. Soccer](https://www.foxnews.com/category/sports/soccer) had no way to appeal the ruing. Article 9.6 of the 2026 World Cup regulations reads, "No protests may be made about the referee’s decisions regarding facts connected with play. Such decisions are final and not subject to appeal, unless otherwise stipulated in the FIFA Disciplinary Code."
![Falorin Balogun celebrates goal](https://a57.foxnews.com/static.foxnews.com/foxnews.com/content/uploads/2026/07/686/384/falorin-balogun-lebron-ode.jpg?ve=1&tl=1)

United States' Folarin Balogun celebrates after scoring the opening goal during the World Cup round of 32 soccer match between the United States and Bosnia in Santa Clara, Calif., near San Francisco, Wednesday, July 1, 2026. (AP Photo/Jeff Chiu)

blocks.md: [**CLICK HERE TO DOWNLOAD THE FOX NEWS APP**](https://foxnews.onelink.me/xLDS?pid=AppArticleLink&af_dp=foxnewsaf%3A%2F%2F&af_web_dp=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.foxnews.com%2Fapps-products)
"If a player or team official is sent off as a result of a direct or indirect red card (second caution), they will automatically be suspended from their team’s subsequent match," Article 10.5 adds.
*Fox News' Jonathan Hunt contributed to this report.*
Ryan Gaydos is a senior editor for Fox News Digital.

doc.description: Folarin Balogun will play for the USA against Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16 after the red card he received against Bosnia and Herzegovina was suspended.
doc.title: Folarin Balogun cleared to play vs. Belgium after red card lifted | Fox News
